# FerroDruid Individual Contributor License Agreement

Version 1.0 (2026)

Thank you for your interest in contributing to FerroDruid (the "Project"),
a project of abyo software 合同会社 (abyo software LLC), a limited liability
company organized under the laws of Japan ("abyo software", "we", "us").

This Individual Contributor License Agreement (this "Agreement") documents
the rights You grant to abyo software in Your Contributions. It protects You
as a contributor as well as abyo software and users of the Project. This
Agreement does not change Your rights to use Your own Contributions for any
other purpose.

**How this Agreement is accepted.** This Agreement is accepted
electronically. When You open Your first pull request against the FerroDruid
repository, CLA Assistant will ask You to read this Agreement and confirm
Your acceptance using Your GitHub account. By using the CLA Assistant
acceptance control while authenticated to Your GitHub account, You
affirmatively accept the exact version of this Agreement displayed or linked
at that time and intend that action to constitute Your electronic signature.
You represent that You control that GitHub account and that the identifying
information You provide is accurate. This Agreement covers all Contributions
submitted by You to the Project before acceptance that remain intended for
inclusion, and all Contributions You submit after acceptance.

abyo software may retain evidence of acceptance, including Your GitHub
username and immutable user ID, the Agreement version and cryptographic
hash, the date and time, the associated pull request, and the recorded
acceptance event.

Except for the licenses granted in this Agreement, You reserve all right,
title, and interest in and to Your Contributions.

## 1. Definitions

"You" (or "Your") means the copyright owner, or the individual authorized by
the copyright owner, that is entering into this Agreement with abyo
software.

"Contribution" means any original work of authorship, including any
modifications or additions to an existing work, that is intentionally
submitted by You to abyo software for inclusion in, or documentation of, the
Project. For the purposes of this definition, "submitted" means any form of
electronic, verbal, or written communication sent to abyo software or its
representatives, including but not limited to pull requests, patches, source
code, documentation, and issue comments submitted through the Project's
repository or communication channels managed by, or on behalf of, abyo
software — but excluding any communication that is conspicuously marked or
otherwise designated in writing by You as "Not a Contribution."

"Work" means the FerroDruid software and its documentation owned or managed
by abyo software, to which Contributions are submitted.

## 2. Grant of Copyright License

Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, You hereby grant to
abyo software a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge,
royalty-free, irrevocable license, with the unrestricted right to grant
sublicenses through one or more tiers, under all copyrights and equivalent
rights that You own or control in the Contributions. This grant expressly
includes all rights provided by Articles 21 through 28 of the Copyright Act
of Japan and corresponding rights under the laws of any jurisdiction,
including the rights to use, reproduce, modify, translate, adapt, arrange,
create and exploit derivative works, publicly perform, publicly display,
present on screen, recite, exhibit, transmit or communicate to the public
(including making available for transmission), distribute, transfer, rent,
import, export, and otherwise exploit the Contributions and derivative
works thereof, in source-code, object-code, documentation, or any other
form.

abyo software may exercise and sublicense those rights, separately or as
part of the Work, commercially or non-commercially, with or without charge,
under one or more licenses concurrently or successively, including: (a) the
Business Source License 1.1 as configured for the Project, including its
Additional Use Grant and its automatic grant of rights under the Apache
License, Version 2.0 on the applicable Change Date; (b) proprietary or
commercial terms, including offerings distributed through AWS Marketplace;
and (c) any present or future open-source, source-available, proprietary,
or other license terms selected by abyo software. No further consent from
You is required for any such licensing or relicensing.

## 3. Grant of Patent License

Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, You hereby grant to
abyo software a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge,
royalty-free, irrevocable (except as stated in this section) patent
license, with the right to grant sublicenses directly or through multiple

If a person or entity receiving a patent license under this Section
institutes patent litigation against any person or entity, including by
cross-claim or counterclaim, alleging that the Work or a Contribution
incorporated in the Work directly or indirectly infringes a patent, all

## 4. Moral Rights

To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, You irrevocably consent
to, and agree not to assert or exercise against abyo software, its
affiliates, successors, licensees, sublicensees, distributors, or
recipients, any moral rights, rights of attribution, rights of integrity,
rights of disclosure or publication, or analogous personal rights that You
have in a Contribution, insofar as their acts are authorized by this
Agreement. This covenant includes consent to modification, translation,
adaptation, combination with other works, publication or non-publication,
and use with, without, or under a changed attribution notice. If any such
right cannot validly be made subject to a non-exercise covenant, You grant
all specific consents permitted by law and agree to provide any further
confirmation reasonably necessary to permit the uses authorized by this
Agreement.

## 5. Your Representations

1. You represent that You are legally entitled to grant the above licenses.

2. If Your employer(s) has rights to intellectual property that You create
   that includes Your Contributions, You represent that You have received
   permission to make Contributions on behalf of that employer, that Your
   employer has waived such rights for Your Contributions to the Project, or
   that Your employer has executed with abyo software a separate Corporate
   Contributor License Agreement covering Your Contributions.

3. You represent that each of Your Contributions is Your original creation
   (see Section 7 for submissions on behalf of others).

4. You represent that Your Contribution submissions include complete details
   of any third-party license or other restriction (including, but not
   limited to, related patents and trademarks) of which You are personally
   aware and which are associated with any part of Your Contributions.

5. You agree to notify abyo software of any facts or circumstances of which
   You become aware that would make these representations inaccurate in any
   respect.

## 6. Support; Disclaimer of Warranty

You are not expected to provide support for Your Contributions, except to
the extent You desire to provide support. You may provide support for free,
for a fee, or not at all. Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in
writing, You provide Your Contributions on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT
WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied, including,
without limitation, any warranties or conditions of TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT,
MERCHANTABILITY, or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

## 7. Submissions That Are Not Your Original Creation

Should You wish to submit work that is not Your original creation, You may
submit it to abyo software separately from any Contribution, identifying the
complete details of its source and of any license or other restriction
(including, but not limited to, related patents, trademarks, and license
agreements) of which You are personally aware, and conspicuously marking the
work as "Submitted on behalf of a third party: [named here]".

## 8. Governing Law; Jurisdiction; Severability

This Agreement is governed by, and shall be construed in accordance with,
the laws of Japan, without regard to its conflict-of-law rules. The Tokyo
District Court has exclusive jurisdiction as the court of first instance
over any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement.

If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, it
will be enforced to the maximum extent permitted by law, and the remaining
provisions will remain in effect.
